resolutions.reason: All repository reads now pass through realpath containment under the operator-selected root.
Directory enumeration is confined before the filesystem sink.
Candidate files are resolved and confined before metadata access.
Candidate files are resolved and confined before metadata access.
The skills directory must resolve inside the selected repository before enumeration.
The skills directory must resolve inside the selected repository before enumeration.
Each SKILL.md realpath must remain inside the selected repository.
The harness source read uses the confined repository reader.
Repository metadata checks use a confined canonical path.
Eval-corpus enumeration starts from a confined canonical directory.
Eval case children are confined before metadata access.
Eval case children are confined before metadata access.
The fallback eval directory is confined before metadata access.
--baseline PATH is an explicit local-operator read capability, not repository-controlled data; the default baseline is separately root-confined.
--write-baseline PATH is an explicit local-operator output capability; the default path is now root-confined.
Writing the operator-selected baseline path is the documented command behavior, without shell evaluation.
Metadata access applies to the same explicit operator-selected baseline capability.
Live-state paths are confined to the selected repository before file metadata or reads.
The owner-map path is confined before metadata and content access.
The lease directory is confined before enumeration.
Each lease child is confined before reading.
--audit JSON_OUT is an explicit local-operator output capability.
Writing the explicitly selected audit output is the documented behavior and uses no shell.
--json PATH is an explicit local-operator output capability.
Writing the explicitly selected JSON output is the documented behavior and uses no shell.
An explicit --html PATH is operator-authorized; the no-argument default is independently confined and rejects symlink escape.
Writing an explicit HTML output is intended; the root-derived default can no longer write outside the selected repository.
The eval runner intentionally supplies a disposable sandbox and the grader checks one fixed candidate filename.
The sample log is a fixed filename inside the eval-runner-selected disposable sandbox.
Reading the fixed sample log is the objective grader's test-only contract.
The missing-file probe uses a fixed filename inside the eval-runner-selected disposable sandbox.
The host cwd ancestor walk now checks the .git marker with lexists and never follows its final symlink or reads content.
The existence sink now receives the canonical in-root path returned by the guard's confinement check.
Lease reads and writes use private_state with an explicit lease root after basename sanitization.
The candidate .git path is fail-safe and its pointer cannot expand authority.
Unexpected .git shapes remain protected rather than certifying staleness.
The untrusted pointer is parsed only to validate it against the checkout's admin namespace.
Only an in-namespace missing admin directory may positively certify a deregistered stale worktree.
The owner map is read through private_state with the harness state directory as explicit root.
The host transcript must be a regular non-link JSONL file before metadata access.
The transcript bucket is reached only through a validated host file capability.
Directory entries are basenames returned by listdir and peer symlinks/junctions are skipped.
Peer metadata is read only for a non-link JSONL child of the validated bucket.
Worktree containment now precedes target probing and lexists avoids following a final symlink.
The optional primary source must remain inside the primary root after realpath or the reviewed remote is used.
The target parent is rechecked after creation before clone execution.
The test runner passes its own temporary checkout path to a test-only coverage contract.
The test copies a reviewed hook into a test-runner-owned temporary fixture.
The test copies a reviewed helper into a test-runner-owned temporary fixture.
